proposed_base_material_variables.crystal_structure: The fundamental crystallographic phase arrangement of the alloy matrix and precipitates (e.g., FCC, BCC) determined via techniques like XRD, which governs structural stability and localized corrosion susceptibility.

proposed_conditioned_material_variables.specimen_dimensions: The specific physical dimensions and geometry (e.g., length, width, thickness) of the prepared material specimen, which define the available surface area for testing and normalization.

proposed_experiment_variables.corrosion_potential: The free corrosion potential or open-circuit potential (Ecorr) established by the material in the electrolyte, serving as the baseline thermodynamic state prior to anodic pitting breakdown.
proposed_experiment_variables.corrosion_current_density: The baseline uniform corrosion rate (icorr) measured in current per unit area, typically extracted from the Tafel region of the polarization curve.
proposed_experiment_variables.polarization_potential_range: The specific starting (initial) and ending (final) electrical potentials applied during the potentiodynamic scan, establishing the boundary conditions of the test.
proposed_experiment_variables.test_standard_protocol: The official standardized methodology or recognized industry standard (e.g., ASTM G59) adhered to for equipment calibration and execution of the electrochemical corrosion experiments.
